thesis: Costco is one of the highest-quality retail businesses ever constructed: a wide-moat membership flywheel with 92.9% renewal rates, 25-30% ROIC over a decade, and structural cost advantages (SKU discipline, negative working capital, supplier leverage) that have strengthened over 40 years. The fundamental business case for ownership is compelling on any 10-year horizon. However, at $952/share (48x normalized P/E, 134% premium to peer EV/EBITDA, DCF base case $535), the price already reflects a near-best-case outcome. New capital entering today earns roughly 2.1% earnings yield vs a 4.3% risk-free rate — requiring either sustained earnings compounding above market-implied 12%/yr or acceptance of index-level returns with single-name concentration risk. The verdict is Hold for existing holders and staged-entry initiation for new buyers, not a high-urgency Add.
valuation_basis: vs normalized 10-year earnings power: normalized FCF/share $17.20 (10-year avg); DCF at 8.4% WACC — intrinsic value bear $398, base $535, bull $760 vs current $952. Normalized P/E 48.2x vs 10-year peer median 27.4x. Not TTM-driven.

kill_criteria.trigger: Worldwide membership renewal rate falls below 89% for two consecutive quarters
kill_criteria.lagging_indicator: Membership fee income growth decelerates below 3%/yr

kill_criteria.trigger: Gross merchandise margin declines more than 150bps from FY2024 baseline (12.7% → below 11.2%) for two consecutive years
kill_criteria.lagging_indicator: Management introduces promotional discounting or loyalty programs inconsistent with the always-low-price model

kill_criteria.trigger: ROIC falls below 18% for two consecutive years (from 29.4% FY2024)
kill_criteria.lagging_indicator: Operating income growth persistently lags revenue growth for 3+ consecutive years

kill_criteria.trigger: Two consecutive new-market international failures: warehouse closures, write-downs > $200M, or <50% target-year enrollment in new countries
kill_criteria.lagging_indicator: International revenue share declines as a % of total for 3 consecutive years

kill_criteria.trigger: Membership fee increase leads to renewal rate drop > 300bps in the 4 quarters following the increase
kill_criteria.lagging_indicator: Total active cardholders shrink YoY (currently 133M, FY2024)

key_risks: Multiple compression: 48x P/E to 35x P/E is a 27% price decline with no fundamental change required
Membership renewal rate mean-reversion from peak 92.9%: each 1pp decline is ~$65-130M in fee income impairment
E-commerce disruption: Amazon Fresh / Whole Foods sustained market share gains in grocery could reduce warehouse visit frequency below the value-perception threshold
International execution risk: India and next-phase China entry face regulatory and logistics challenges
Risk-free rate competition: at 4.3% risk-free vs 2.1% earnings yield, the opportunity cost of holding equities at this multiple is meaningful

citations.claim: Normalized FCF/share $17.20; DCF bear $398 / base $535 / bull $760
Wide moat score 7/10 — cost advantage (2), switching costs (2), network effects (1), intangible assets (1), efficient scale (1)
citations.url_or_id: CIK 0000909832 / 10-K FY2024 + web research; see moat.json

dissent_summary: Conservative debator argued for 25-30% initial position (not 50%) on grounds that 48x P/E leaves no cushion for multiple compression and the risk-free vs earnings yield spread is unusually punishing. This was partially accepted: the sizing guidance is staged-tranche at 50% Stage 1 rather than the 75% that the bull case would support, as a concession to conservative's valuation concern. Bear argued verdict should be Trim for existing holders at current valuations — overridden because Costco's business quality and compounding characteristics mean exiting at 48x in a business growing earnings 12%/yr would be a poor long-term decision for existing holders with a long horizon and embedded gains.
